relationships.description: Best friend and bowling partner — volatile, loyal, and disruptive
Feminist artist who uses the Dude for her own purposes
The millionaire whose identity the Dude is confused for
Best friend who makes everything worse by trying to help
Bowling teammate whom Walter constantly berates
Uses the Dude as a patsy in his embezzlement scheme
Young trophy wife whose 'kidnapping' drives the plot

characters.name: Maude Lebowski
motivations: conceive a child with no father figure obligations

relationships.description: Uses the Dude as means to an end, but with mutual respect
Daughter who sees through her father's pretensions
Constantly told to shut up by Walter
Trophy wife who wasn't actually kidnapped

places.name: The Dude's Bungalow
places.description: A shabby Venice Beach bungalow with a rug that really ties the room together.

places.name: The Bowling Alley
places.description: Hollywood Star Lanes — the Dude's true home, where the league competes and White Russians flow.

places.name: The Lebowski Mansion
places.description: The Big Lebowski's ostentatious Pasadena estate — all surface grandeur hiding fraud.

places.name: Malibu Beach / Jackie Treehorn's
places.description: The decadent Malibu party world where the Dude is drugged and the plot spirals further.

objects.name: The Rug
objects.description: A rug urinated on by nihilist thugs who mistook the Dude for the Big Lebowski.
objects.significance: The inciting object — 'it really tied the room together.' Its loss sets everything in motion.
rules: Its ruination is a case of mistaken identity

objects.name: The Ransom Briefcase
objects.description: A briefcase supposedly containing $1 million for Bunny's ransom — actually stuffed with dirty underwear by Walter.
objects.significance: Everyone chases this briefcase, but it never contains what anyone thinks it does.
rules: Walter substituted dirty laundry for the money
The real money was embezzled by the Big Lebowski

objects.name: Bowling Ball
objects.description: The bowling ball — constant through all the chaos, representing the Dude's one stable passion.
objects.significance: Bowling is the one thing that remains constant. The tournament matters more to the Dude than the mystery.
rules: The bowling subplot continues regardless of the crime plot

moments.label: The Dude Abides
moments.description: The Dude lives his contented, slovenly life — bowling, White Russians, and his beloved rug.

char_dude.wants: to be left alone and bowl
knows: nothing about the Big Lebowski or kidnapping plots

moments.label: The Rug Incident
moments.description: Nihilist thugs urinate on the Dude's rug, mistaking him for the millionaire Lebowski. Walter convinces the Dude to seek compensation.

transitions.description: His rug — which really tied the room together — is ruined
Goes to the Big Lebowski to demand compensation

moments.label: The Kidnapping Caper
moments.description: The Dude is pulled into a fake kidnapping, a botched ransom drop, nihilists, Maude's schemes, and increasingly absurd encounters.

transitions.detail: Bunny has been 'kidnapped' and the Big Lebowski wants the Dude to deliver ransom
The briefcase money vanishes — Walter substituted dirty underwear

moments.label: Donny's Heart Attack
moments.description: After a confrontation with the nihilists, Donny suffers a heart attack and dies. The senseless loss grounds the comedy in real grief.

transitions.description: Heart attack during the nihilist confrontation
Loses his quiet, gentle bowling companion

moments.label: The Truth Revealed
moments.description: Bunny was never kidnapped — she was on vacation. The Big Lebowski embezzled the ransom. The whole thing was a scam.

transitions.description: Realizes the entire kidnapping was a fraud
Exposed as a fraud who embezzled from his own charity

moments.label: Scattering Donny's Ashes
moments.description: The Dude and Walter scatter Donny's ashes at the beach. Walter's eulogy goes wrong, but they reconcile through shared grief.

transitions.description: Reconnects with Walter through shared loss and absurdity

moments.label: The Dude Abides (Again)
moments.description: The Dude returns to bowling. Nothing has changed and everything has changed. The Dude abides.

transitions.description: Returns to his life with the same easy-going nature, having survived the chaos
